title: [$250] Make the group headers appear sticky when scrolling down the Spend table

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

body:
When you expand a group header from Spend, as soon as you scroll down the page the group header goes out of view. This means you lose context of what group you are viewing. Let's make it so that when you scroll down, the group header stays sticky ([like a standard sticky header component](https://codepen.io/chalarangelo/pen/ExqjMaP)) but scrolls with the page once content below should push it back up. I tried to make a quick POC but the result isn't perfect, but it communicates the idea: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28082b6b-6165-48c0-ac8e-a11ccda4303a Missing would be: - there is a slight 4px shift of the sticky rows, we want to make sure the header rows appear as tall as they do (56px) when they are sticky vs not sticky - I actually think we might want the child table's header to stay fixed as well and use the same sticky behavior as the group header cc @Expensify/design @Expensify/product @Krishna2323 @JS00001 This is going to he handled by Krishna since they are currently working on report table styles. Jack mentioned maybe `stickyHeaderIndices` will be our friend here.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Krishna2323

title: [$250] Native: keep custom amount numpad fixed when currency search shows system keyboard

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem On iOS/Android, on the money request **amount** step, opening the **currency** picker and focusing **search** shows the native keyboard. Keyboard-avoiding layout on the amount screen shifts the **custom numpad** above the keyboard; when the picker closes, the numpad jumps back. ## Expected The custom numpad stays in its normal bottom position (the system keyboard may overlay it during search). No visible **jump** when opening or closing the currency picker. ## Repro 1. Start money request → amount step. 2. Open currency picker → focus search (native keyboard). 3. See numpad move up; dismiss picker and see it snap back. ## Implementation hints - [`src/pages/iou/request/step/IOURequestStepAmount.tsx`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/iou/request/step/IOURequestStepAmount.tsx) - [`src/pages/iou/request/step/StepScreenWrapper.tsx`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/iou/request/step/StepScreenWrapper.tsx) (`shouldEnableKeyboardAvoidingView`) - [`src/pages/iou/MoneyRequestAmountForm.tsx`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/pages/iou/MoneyRequestAmountForm.tsx) / `BigNumberPad` Also check other screens that combine `MoneyRequestAmountForm` + currency modal. Validate on small devices after any change.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @aimane-chnaif

title: [$250] Advanced Approval Routing Inconsistencies Across Category/Tag Rules, Multiple Transactions, and Self-Approval Fallback

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.62-3 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @parasharrajat **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1776709424725469) ## Action Performed: ### Test Case 4: Category-Based Rule Approver **Setup:** 1.  Use a Control workspace with **Advanced Approval** enabled 2.  Go to **Workspace > Categories** and create categories: `Travel`, `Office Supplies` 3.  Go to **Workspace > Rules** (or Approval Rules config) and add a rule:     -   **Category = `Travel`** → Approver: **Approver D** 4.  Configure User A → `submitsTo: Approver B` 5.  No tag-based rules **Test (category rule matches):** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create an expense categorized as `Travel`, submit the report 3.  **Verify:** Report routes to **Approver D** (the category rule approver), NOT Approver B 4.  **Verify:** The optimistic managerID shows Approver D immediately 5.  Check in Classic that the state is consistent **Test (category rule does not match):** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create an expense categorized as `Office Supplies` (no rule for this category), submit 3.  **Verify:** Report routes to **Approver B** (falls back to submitsTo since no rule matches) * * * ### Test Case 5: Tag-Based Rule Approver **Setup:** 1.  Use a Control workspace with **Advanced Approval** enabled 2.  Go to **Workspace > Tags** and create tags: `Project Alpha`, `Project Beta` 3.  Add an approval rule:     -   **Tag = `Project Alpha`** → Approver: **Approver D** 4.  Configure User A → `submitsTo: Approver B` 5.  No category-based rules **Test:** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create an expense tagged `Project Alpha`, submit the report 3.  **Verify:** Report routes to **Approver D** (the tag rule approver) 4.  **Verify:** The optimistic managerID shows Approver D immediately * * * ### Test Case 6: Category Rule Takes Priority Over Tag Rule **Setup:** 1.  Use a Control workspace with **Advanced Approval** enabled 2.  Add approval rules:     -   **Category = `Travel`** → Approver: **Approver C**     -   **Tag = `Project Alpha`** → Approver: **Approver D** 3.  Configure User A → `submitsTo: Approver B` **Test:** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create a **single** expense with category `Travel` AND tag `Project Alpha`, submit 3.  **Verify:** Report routes to **Approver C** (category rule takes priority over tag rule) 4.  **Verify:** The optimistic managerID shows Approver C * * * ### Test Case 7: Multiple Transactions with Different Category Approvers **Setup:** 1.  Use a Control workspace with **Advanced Approval** enabled 2.  Add approval rules:     -   **Category = `Travel`** → Approver: **Approver C**     -   **Category = `Meals`** → Approver: **Approver D** 3.  Configure User A → `submitsTo: Approver B` **Test:** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create **two** expenses on the same report:     -   Expense 1: category = `Travel` (Approver C)     -   Expense 2: category = `Meals` (Approver D) 3.  Submit the report 4.  **Verify:** Report routes to the approver matching the **first** transaction (by created date). `getSubmitToAccountID` uses `getFirstRuleApprover` which breaks on the first category match in sorted transaction order 5.  **Verify:** The optimistic managerID matches the API managerAccountID (both from `getSubmitToAccountID`) * * * ### Test Case 8: Rule Approver Is the Submitter (Self-Approval Scenario) **Setup:** 1.  Use a Control workspace with **Advanced Approval** enabled 2.  Add an approval rule:     -   **Category = `Travel`** → Approver: **User A** (the submitter themselves!) 3.  Configure User A → `submitsTo: Approver B` **Test:** 1.  Log in as User A 2.  Create an expense categorized as `Travel`, submit 3.  **Verify:** Report routes to **Approver B** (NOT User A), because `getFirstRuleApprover` skips self-approvers and falls back to `getManagerAccountID` 4.  **Verify:** The optimistic managerID shows Approver B ## Expected Result: Approval routing should: Follow clear and deterministic logic: Category rule (highest priority) Tag rule Fallback to submitsTo Skip self-approval and fallback correctly Use consistent logic across frontend and backend Optimistic UI should: Always match backend response ## Actual Result: Potential inconsistencies in: Rule matching and fallback behavior Priority handling between category and tag rules Multi-transaction routing logic (first-match dependency) Self-approval fallback Optimistic vs actual approver mismatch ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos Test Case 7: Multiple Transactions with Different Category Approvers https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3bbcacc-22d6-4335-9e73-538a39888957 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Set up billing with a designated billing owner account. 2. Waited for the scheduled billing cycle (April 1st). 3. Checked the billing owner account for the automatically created expense. 4. Verified that no expense was generated for the April 1st billing. ## Expected Result: The system should automatically create an expense for the April 1st bill under the billing owner account as part of the scheduled billing process. ## Actual Result: No expense was created for the April 1st bill under the billing owner account. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777047538082199) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.62-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes. Repro only in Honor x6b/ Android 14 **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87089 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** N/A **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Honor x6b/ Android 14 **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: 1. For a new account, complete onboarding with "Manage my team's expenses" 2. Tap on the workspace > Overview page 3. Tab the Invite member button 4. Enter and select an email address 5. Tap Next > Tap Invite 6. Tab the Invite member button 7. Add a email address > Next Result: App crashes 8. Launch app 9. Tap on the workspace > Overview page 10. Tab the Invite member button 11. Add a email address > Next Result: App crashes 12. Repeat step 8 to 11 Result: App crashes ## Expected Result: User should land on the Invite message screen without any crash ## Actual Result: App crashes ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/45d5fa60-512c-4d7a-98de-30cec41998d5 [dumpstate_log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27067141/dumpstate_log.txt) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Nested table rows no longer highlight on hover or open expense in RHP on click

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

body:
Nested table rows in the report view no longer highlight on hover, and clicking the row no longer opens the expense in the RHP. Users must now click the `>` chevron tap area exactly to open the expense. This is a regression likely introduced by the compact table styles update (https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/86283). **Parent issue:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/86102 **Related draft PR:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88296 **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1776703628974419
